properties.remark: Aroostook EMA has reported several washouts on Church Ave in Frenchville. Road is closed.
Social media photo showing excessive runoff flooding Route 1 at the railroad tracks.
Corrects previous flood report from 1 NE Frenchville. Corrected flooding type from flood to flash flood. Flooding reported at Starbarn Ave and US Route 1.
Photos via Social Media. Excessive runoff flooding portion of Route 1 west of Frenchville.
Piscataquis County Sheriffs Office reports Flash Flooding closing portion of Greenville Road in Monson.
Piscataquis County Sheriffs Office reports Flash Flooding closing portion of Elliotsville Road in Monson.
Corrects previous flood report from Monson. Corrected flood type from flood to Flash Flood. Indian Point Loop in Monson is reported to be flooded. Report was relayed to local law enforcement.
Several trees down with large tree down on wires blocking the Cooper Road (N. Union Road) approx 1mi north of Route 191. Photo via social media.
Three trees blown down.
A couple of trees down in Marion Township. Time estimated via radar.
Large tree down on wires blocking the Cooper Road.
Corrects previous tstm wnd dmg report from 2 S Alexander. Large tree down on wires blocking the Cooper Road (N. Union Road) approx 1mi north of Route 191. Photo via social media.
Large tree down on wires blocking the Cooper Road (N. Union Road) approx 1mi north of Route 191. Photo via social media.
Tree down on power line in Garfield Plantation. Time estimated based on radar.
Delayed Report. Flash Flood of the Nesowadnehunk Stream due to Thunderstorms. Park rangers report the Park Tote Road was flooded with 8-10 inches of running water.
Correction to Thunderstorm Wind Damage. Large tree down blocking Katahdin Iron Works Road (North Woods Road) at the 3rd bridge past the checkpoint. Time estimated by radar.
Tree down on powerlines. Power is out in the area. Time estimated on radar.
Corrects previous tstm wnd dmg report from 1 NNW Brownville Junction. Tree down on powerlines. Power is out in the area. Time estimated on radar.
Tree down on powerlines blocking Davis St (Route 11). Power is out in the area. Time estimated based on radar.
Route 16 closed south of Sebec. Road beginning to wash out.
Several trees down on the northeast corner of Cross Lake, including one on a house and another on a power line.
estimated hail size from Facebook photo.
Corrects previous hail report from Wellington.
Multiple trees down, including a large tree down on a roof causing damage to a roof and solar panels. Tree limbs down on lines causing a power outage.
several smaller trees down across the town.
Very large tree across road; chainsaw needed to remove.
Delayed Report. Photos via social media show a large tree snapped at the base blocking Hampden Rd in Carmel.
Multiple gravel roads washed out north of Wellington.
Trees and wires down near Charleston. Timing based on radar.
Delayed report. Trees and wires down along Pine Tree Road.
Trees fallen on power lines. Also flooding along one lane of Kenduskeag Rd in Levant.
Photo via social media of ping pong ball sized hail in Charleston. Time estimated by radar.
Multiple downed and split trees for about a mile along RT 11 near Bradford. Time estimated from radar.
Delayed Report. Tree down blocking road near Charleston Correctional Facility.
Tree down on power lines in Bradford.
Delayed Report via social media. Tree down on power lines blocking Middle Road. Time estimated by radar.
Delayed Report. Charleston Fire Dept advises multiple trees down blocking Bradford Road and downed powerlines.
Delayed report. Photo via social media of half dollar sized hail. Time estimated by Radar.

Photos via social media show numerous trees down on powerlines. At least 20 trees are down or on powerlines between Route 11A split and Bradford General Store. Power remains out in the area as of 4:50AM Aug 14th. Time of damage estimated by radar.

Maine Forest Service conducted an areal survey of Lovejoy Ridge along the Aroostook & Piscataquis border. They found a dozen mature large maple/sugarbush trees snapped at the base or uprooted. The trees were fanned out indicating straight line wind damage. Estimated 60mph winds. Time estimated by radar.
Water across road with washout near Cornith Rd and Fernald Rd in Garland.
Delayed Report. Garland Road partially blocked and washed out from overwhelmed culverts. Additionally multiple newer culverts washed out on Mill Road along the Garland/Charleston town line.
Water over Kenduskeag Rd and Sawyer Rd near the Levant/Kenduskeag Line.
Water running across Garland Rd.
Delayed Report. Puddledock Rd in the area of Paine Rd flooded and impassable.
Delayed Report. Harris Rd in Charleston closed and impassable due to flash flooding.
Road washout on Bradford Rd between Main Rd and Puddledock Rd.
Several trees reported down across River Road in Oakfield.
Partial road closure of Route 214 at Craigs Brook. This is between Charlotte Fire Station and Sherrard Lane.
Charlotte Vol. Fire & Rescue reports Fisher Brook has flooded Station Road in the area of Goodeill Road. Road is closed.
Charlotte Rd in vicinity of Round Pound is partially closed due to flash flooding.
Sunrise ATV trail washed out at milepost 64.
Partial washout of culvert on Reversing Falls Rd near intersection with Cemetery Rd.
One side of road washed out. Road closed.
Ice Jam forming behind island just upstream of Mill Dam. Ice still solid but rotting upstream of Guilford.
Ice jam starts from the bend in Fort Fairfield and extends about 2 miles upstream, past the Fort Fairfield bridge. Boat landing is currently impacted but roads remain dry.
1 mile down from NEPP site, 1.5 mile long ice jam. No flooding, water moving through the ice jam.
Ice jam downstream of Dickey Bridge, through Allagash and St Francis. No current flooding reported.
Corrects previous tstm wnd gst report from 8 NE Charlotte.
